    1. outboxOpenTelemetrySdk(Environment, OutboxProperties)

Class OutboxOpenTelemetrySdkAutoConfiguration

java.lang.Object
io.github.lobofoltran.outbox.spring.OutboxOpenTelemetrySdkAutoConfiguration
@AutoConfiguration(before=OutboxAutoConfiguration.class) @ConditionalOnClass({OpenTelemetry.class,io.opentelemetry.sdk.autoconfigure.AutoConfiguredOpenTelemetrySdk.class}) @ConditionalOnProperty(prefix="io.github.lobofoltran.outbox.tracing.sdk", name="enabled") @EnableConfigurationProperties(OutboxProperties.class) public class OutboxOpenTelemetrySdkAutoConfiguration extends Object
Bridges Spring's Environment into AutoConfiguredOpenTelemetrySdk so otel.* keys placed in application.yml (or any other PropertySource) are honored by the SDK.

Background — DEBT-12: the upstream opentelemetry-sdk-extension-autoconfigure consults system properties, environment variables, and a classpath .properties file. It does not read Spring's Environment, so an adopter who configures 

otel:
  service:
    name: my-service
  exporter:
    otlp:
      endpoint: http://collector:4317
in application.yml sees every span tagged service.name=unknown_service:java. There is no Spring-Boot-aware variant of the SDK autoconfigure in the official OpenTelemetry Java repository; this autoconfig closes the gap with a minimal PropertiesSupplier bridge.

When this autoconfig publishes a bean

  • opentelemetry-sdk-extension-autoconfigure is on the classpath (AutoConfiguredOpenTelemetrySdk resolves),
  • io.github.lobofoltran.outbox.tracing.sdk.enabled is explicitly set to true (opt-in; the bridge stays inert by default because AutoConfiguredOpenTelemetrySdk defaults every signal exporter to OTLP and would fail context startup if opentelemetry-exporter-otlp is absent from the classpath),
  • no other OpenTelemetry bean has been registered. Adopters wiring OpenTelemetry through the Java Agent or opentelemetry-spring-boot-starter are not overridden.

The resulting OpenTelemetry bean is consumed by the tracing decorator in OutboxAutoConfiguration. The bean factory declares destroyMethod = "close" so the SDK's shutdown hook (flush exporters, close span processors) runs on context shutdown — the return type is the OpenTelemetry interface to avoid a hard compile dependency on opentelemetry-sdk, and Spring resolves the close() method reflectively against the runtime instance.

Global state

The SDK is not installed as the JVM-wide GlobalOpenTelemetry by default. The outbox tracing decorator consumes the bean directly, and mutating the global can collide with the OTel Java Agent. Adopters who need GlobalOpenTelemetry.get() (or downstream instrumentation libraries that rely on it) to return this SDK should set io.github.lobofoltran.outbox.tracing.sdk.set-as-global=true.

Since:
0.5.0

    Builds an
    invalid reference 
    OpenTelemetrySdk
    via AutoConfiguredOpenTelemetrySdk, seeded with every otel.* property visible to Spring's Environment.

  • Constructor Details

    • OutboxOpenTelemetrySdkAutoConfiguration

      public OutboxOpenTelemetrySdkAutoConfiguration()

  • Method Details

    • outboxOpenTelemetrySdk

      @Bean(destroyMethod="close") @ConditionalOnMissingBean(OpenTelemetry.class) public OpenTelemetry outboxOpenTelemetrySdk(org.springframework.core.env.Environment environment, OutboxProperties properties)
      Builds an
      invalid reference 
      OpenTelemetrySdk
      via AutoConfiguredOpenTelemetrySdk, seeded with every otel.* property visible to Spring's Environment.

      Spring properties act as defaults: the SDK's normal precedence (system properties > env vars > properties supplier) is preserved, so a CLI flag or container env var still wins over a value baked into application.yml.

      Parameters:
      environment - Spring's environment, scanned for otel.* keys.
      properties - bound outbox properties; only the tracing.sdk sub-record is read.
      Returns:
      the configured SDK bean.
      Since:
      0.5.0
